reviews.review_text: its incredibly pathetic that EA tries to force you to install their app to play this game. I would not have bought this game if I had known this the case. However if you have already paid for it you can get around that by navigating to the game folder/swgame/binaries/win64 and launching swgamewin64shipping.exe in there. Pin it to your desktop, and go to hell Electronic Arts.
Beautiful story, charming characters, and really good ponchos

--Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order--

You embark on a journey as Cal Kestis trying to rebuild the Jedi Order whilst facing the wrath of the Dark Side. Overall the game is very straightforward in its objectives but requires backtracking to the same planets you have already visited to uncover more secrets. The gameplay is good, presentation is really solid and the story is alright if you are a casual fan or can be a good hook if you enjoy the Star Wars universe!

The achievement grind for the completionist out there is however on the frustrating end as there are no fast travel points in the game and even a single missed collectible can require minutes of backtracking and confusion. The achievements really boils down to collectibles and following the right path in to getting it. Following a guide is highly advisable if you don't plan to play it again. 

My Ratings:

Excluding the 100% achievements: 8/10
Including the 100% achievements: 7/10

This game is one of the best Star Wars games out there. The story is fantastic and the world is a beauty to look at that will have you wrapped into this amazing galaxy far far away. Runs like a charm on my pc on all maxed out settings. I've only finished the story and there is still so much to do ! Highly recommend for even non Star Wars fans. 10/10

Very good game..combat is fun, story is good, platforming is awesome, puzzles are good. Traversing and interconnection of areas are outstanding, but missed fast travel in the game. Although a “souls-lite” but its pretty easy on jedi master difficulty. 
Looking forward to the jedi survivor

Star Wars Jedi; Fallen Order.
Cal's introduction, and out of the handfull of SW game's we've gotten over the years, this still feel's like 1 of the more well done stories. 8)
had to revisit, complete and get my head around before venturing into the next game; but in thought of Cal ever entering "Live Action" would like to be up with his journey, 
Game again is well done; the "Open Map" can get tedious for exploring and well.. if you venture deep, a lil too deep quickly you will find plenty of things you can't get to and will have to return....
so I cbf doing the completionst runs though i'm, sure NG+ would be a banger xD, and who knows maybe I'll get bored enough, revisit, complete and add to the review.
but yeah without saying too much or any spoilers;
 if you're a fan of Star Wars, then I'm sure you'll enjoy the ride

👍 Recommended
🕒 23.7 hours on record
📅 Completed Campaign - 14 February 2026

One of the best Star Wars games I’ve played honestly. The story is really good, the characters are memorable, and Cal’s journey was way more interesting than I expected.

The worlds look beautiful and exploring them really makes you feel like you’re inside the Star Wars universe. Combat is also super satisfying once you get used to it, especially using the lightsaber and Force abilities together.

The game mixes exploration, platforming, puzzles, and combat really well, so it rarely gets boring. Sometimes the map can be a little confusing and backtracking can get annoying if you go too deep into areas before unlocking abilities, but overall it’s still a really fun experience.

Performance on PC was great for me and the graphics look amazing, especially on higher settings. Also, the ponchos are elite.

Even if you’re not the biggest Star Wars fan, I’d still recommend it because it’s just a solid action-adventure game overall. Definitely worth playing before jumping into Jedi Survivor.

I just finished Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order and I loved it. I have always been a huge Star Wars fan, so this game felt something special to me from the beginning. 

What made the game stand out for me was the atmosphere. The planets and story genuinely felt like Star Wars. There were moments where I just stopped playing for a minute to look around because the environments looked so good. 

Cal Kestis ended up becoming one of my favorite Star Wars characters by the end of the game. You really get to see his struggles, fears, and growth throughout the story, which made me care about what happened to him. And BD-1 stole so many scenes for me. He is easily one of the most lovable droids in any Star Wars game.

The game takes place between Episode III and IV, during the time when the Empire is hunting surviving Jedi. Respawn did an amazing job balancing story, exploration, and challenging gameplay.

For me, this is easily one of the best modern Star Wars games. You can tell the people behind it cared about Star Wars, and as a longtime fan, that made the experience even better. I already miss playing it.

Bought it on sale, so worth it for a broke student like me lol, good combat mechanic even tho its really hard once you raise the difficulty to Jedi Master, the story is good, a bit stuck when doing some puzzle but overal its a good game for me.

A fun game with a compelling story. I feel that because I played half the game on the hardest dificulty, which was super unfair where attacks did literally twice as much damage, I got way more frustrated at the gameplay. Parrying felt really good when successful, but on grand master difficulty the windows made parrying feel not worth it most of the time. I wish there was an in between for grand master and jedi knight bc jedi knight was significantly easier than grand master, and grand master felt unfun to play most of the time. I feel like it suffered some with level design because they were unable to keep creating things that felt threatening other than just throwing a million enemies at you. The puzzles were enjoyable but another issue I had was the placement of the rests. other than that, it felt like there was either nothing going on between saves or there was way too much and if you died near the end it was a very long and painful backtrack.

Very competent soulslike gameplay with metroidvania style exploration, both of which were very enjoyable for me. I first played this game 3 years ago and circled back after getting its sequel. Playing both on the "Jedi Master" difficulty, I would say this is the more challenging game, though with a pleasing simplicity compared to some of the complexified mechanics the sequel brings. Deflecting blaster bolts in the effortless jedi style is one area where this game feels more satisfying than its sequel, even allowing you to deflect simply by swinging the lightsaber (e.g. during a melee fight while also getting shot at). Boss fights are another area where this game perhaps wins. It's no Sekiro but they're still a great challenge, while Survivor's boss fights feel a bit too easy most of the time. The story is a simple, well-rounded Star Wars adventure, full of cinematic moments and many elements from the Clone Wars and such that will be appreciated by any dedicated SW fan. The inquisitor character Second Sister is particularly interesting and really well voice acted. All round it's a good experience that I definitely recommend, and not a bad starting point for the soulslike genre.

the puzzles of this game will make you jump off a cliff commit war crimes and end your life 
best game i have ever played
do not pay 40€ for this game. The levels are nice and gameplay is too, but the enemy design is absolutely horrible. If you are going to buy this game, buy it on sale.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a story-driven action-adventure set in the aftermath of Order 66, following former Padawan Cal Kestis as he is forced out of hiding and drawn into a conflict with the Empire. It delivers a highly entertaining Star Wars experience built around strong atmosphere, memorable characters, satisfying progression, and an engaging narrative that captures the feeling of slowly rediscovering what it means to be a Jedi. While the story is not especially groundbreaking and several developments are fairly predictable, the game succeeds through its emotional sincerity, cinematic presentation, and strong sense of adventure.

At its core, the game allows players to explore a series of interconnected planets, progress through a linear main story, solve environmental puzzles, and return to earlier areas after unlocking new abilities. This light Metroidvania structure works well overall, giving exploration a sense of discovery without turning the game into a full open world. Some planets are more memorable than others, with stronger locations offering great set pieces, vertical level design, and a clear sense of danger. The BD-1 holomap can be awkward and confusing at first, but its colored path indicators help prevent navigation from becoming too frustrating.

Combat is enjoyable, especially when the game focuses on lightsaber duels and Force abilities. Cal starts out noticeably weak, but gradually becomes more capable as he reconnects with the Force, making progression feel both mechanically and narratively satisfying. Parries, dodges, stamina management, and enemy attack patterns create a solid combat loop, though the system does not always feel as precise or responsive as it should. Creature-based bosses are often less memorable than duels against lightsaber-wielding enemies, and some encounters feel more clunky than challenging.

The progression system is one of the game’s stronger elements. New Force powers, movement abilities, and combat upgrades make Cal’s growth feel earned, while also opening up new paths across previous planets. The skill tree is not especially deep, but it provides enough meaningful upgrades to keep the player engaged. Cosmetic rewards such as outfits, ship colors, droid skins, and lightsaber parts are a nice addition, though they rarely feel essential. Lightsaber customization is cool, but many rewards are more pleasant extras than strong exploration incentives.

Visually, Fallen Order still holds up well. Its environments feel recognizably Star Wars without relying only on familiar locations, and the game frequently delivers strong cinematic moments. Lightsabers are a major visual highlight, especially on an OLED display, where their glow and color have much stronger impact. Animation quality is more mixed, with some traversal movements and combat transitions lacking the polish of stronger modern action-adventures. Even so, the overall art direction and atmosphere remain very effective.

The audio design is excellent and plays a major role in selling the Star Wars fantasy. The soundtrack, lightsaber effects, blaster sounds, droid noises, and Imperial machinery all feel authentic and immersive. Voice acting is strong across the main cast, with Cal working well as a capable but wounded protagonist. BD-1 stands out as one of the game’s most charming elements, adding personality, humor, and emotional warmth without undermining the darker tone of the story.

Narratively, Fallen Order tells a solid and surprisingly engaging story about survival, trauma, identity, and hope under the shadow of the Empire. Cal’s journey is compelling because he does not begin as a fully formed Jedi hero, but as someone slowly rebuilding his connection to the Force and to his past. The supporting cast adds meaningful emotional depth, and the game balances serious themes with well-placed moments of humor. While the plot itself is not always unpredictable, the character work and atmosphere make the journey memorable.

On the technical side, Fallen Order is a mixed experience. On modern hardware, the game performs well and still looks impressive, but it lacks the polish of the best games in the genre. Bugs, occasional glitches, rough movement behavior, missing indicators, and controller prompt issues can break immersion. These problems do not ruin the experience, but they appear often enough to matter and prevent the game from feeling truly refined.

Final Verdict:
Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a flawed but highly enjoyable action-adventure that succeeds primarily because it understands the fantasy of being a Jedi. Its combat can be clunky, its map is not always intuitive, and its technical issues are noticeable, but the strong atmosphere, memorable characters, satisfying progression, and cinematic Star Wars presentation make it absolutely worth playing. It is not a masterpiece, but it is a very good Star Wars adventure and especially easy to recommend at a discount or as part of a bundle.

Score: 7.5/10
Very fun, great story, and good characters

runs well, is very fun, decent story

great game if you can get it on sale

Fun little game, get it on sale
nice pace, good dificulty, story keeps evolving, a lot of secrets to find, some customization, entertaining
It's a cool casual game with a great story, and it's a lot of fun. I highly recommend it.
Sure, good fun, but i'm tired of developers making me sign in to their app to play the game just so they can spam me with the newest deals.  Screw you EA and UBI and all the rest of you

i just loved this game  ye some bits might be a lil basic like wall run here   ect puzzles ect  but ultimately  it had me loving every bit of being a jedi and when it ended i did not even know it was coming i cant wait to play number 2... i got it on sale 100% worth  it on sale ( get a controller)

Great Game.
as a star wars fan really enjoyed it. But istg their mapping system and the lack of fast travel is so annoying i could have saved hour instead of losing my way in zeffo. 
Now despite these things the storyline, the protagonist, enemies all great
however it's a really fraustrating feeling when EA knows how to handle Jedi better rather than disney-lucasfilm itself

Absolutely the most frustrating, slowest, jankiest combat I have ever seen in a game. That on top of the fact that he is supposedly a Jedi??? What were they thinking. Anyway, story was fine but this guy has no aura
Amazing! Great graphics, story, and combat. Smooth. Worth it!

I really want to like this game. Story is good though I didn't click with Cal. Didn't like him much. Environments are beautiful. Puzzles fun to solve. But this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ combat is so bad. You move like in a jelly, you constantly rubberbanding between enemies and they're too. One on one is mostly ok, but when there are more enemies whole flow goes to ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. You constantly staggered. You can't control a battlefield. Many times enemie just blocked me in a corner and farmed me with staggering attack so i couldn't move from there. Camera not your friend eather. And it feel really bad like you not in control of a character. Attacks badly readable. Final boss was really frustrating for me. I almost dropped the game because combat is so frustratingly poor designed.

I'm almost recommend it.

Very poorly optimized for a game that came out under 10 years ago, I especially was having an issue with audio desyncs during cutscenes (fixed by putting a more restrictive frame lock), but once I got passed that the game was excellent. Id never really played a souls-like before and this seemed like a great introduction. Overall highly recommend if on sale.
I loved this game! The story was great, and the combat was simple yet fun to play. The only issue I have is that the game would crash on occasion.
Great story, great characters, and good fighting mechanics. Such a goated Star Wars single player experience.
Amazing game. Fantastic graphics, great and exciting story & lots to explore even after the main story.

I like Star Wars and I like Dark Souls, so mixing the two together is a recipe for success in my book. The combat feels great, especially when you get the timing down. Cal's arc throughout the game is also fairly solid.
Peak gaming, long enough and super storyline. Makes you hope to see a star wars movie with Cal in it. Second and last playthrough completed.
Freaking bad ass game! the maps and puzzles are a Blast, and the boss fights Force you to up your game!! I highly recommend this game to everyone!!
Really cool game  but definitely on the shorter side of games i play these days! It left me wanting more!
Pretty good, my first "souls-like" (I use that term lightly since this is quite clearly magnitudes easier than souls-games) so it took a whole for the combat to click but I liked it
Jogo com um combate muito divertido, contando também com uma exploração bem recompensadora. Um deleite para quem curte o universo de Star Wars!

I played this on a Macbook Air M4 using Crossover. Performance was okayish, smooth most of the time but lots of stutters.

It's my first ever soulslike game. I enjoyed the game from the start to finish. It has a very engaging narrative. There's a lot of backtracking on planets but its integrated seamlessly so you wouldn't even notice it most of the time. 

It also has a lot of action set pieces that kinda give a tomb raider or uncharted feel but those pieces kinda reduce the monotonous nature of combat. 

Overall, an amazing game.
Exciting battles, fantastic exploration and a gripping story with interesting characters. Definitely worth a try!

Good game, decent story and visuals. Especially good use of the music. Star Wars music is its own genre and many projects under disney just simply ditched it, losing the Star Wars identity. Not the case here. The fighting is a bit clunky, but good enough. My biggest gripe is the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game engine, traversal stutter all the time. My hardware is utilized at around 30% for both cpu and gpu. Disgusting and I hear it might be even worse for the 2nd game. Also just ♥♥♥♥ the ♥♥♥♥♥♥ EA bloatware in general. EA, your platform is a failure, get rid of it!

Anyway, had a good time, glad I picked this up.

Good game design, great customization, runs well on low end hardware.
With some controller settings, it plays like Sekiro.

Game is fantastic. I bought it on EA originally when it came out so please ignore my "playtime" but it was so good I figured I would get it on Steam when it went on sale.

If you enjoy Star Wars and Action/Adventure games, I couldn't recommend this game more! The fact that the difficulty scales from piss easy to practically a souls game means there is a challenge level for absolutely anyone to enjoy!
I enjoyed the worlds, the graphics, but I recommend playing with a controller not a keyboard.

i love this game reminds me a lot of force unleashed from back in the day and i highly recommend it 

but not all is sunshine and rainbows and the remainder of this review is for EA:

dear EA, what ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ super cocaine were you on when you ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ brain dead mouth breathing ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ decided that a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ parkour soulslike star wars game needed ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ puzzles? That is probably one of if not the worst part about this game is the unneeded amount of puzzles. Furthermore the fights themselves aint even challenging for the basic enemy types (which im not needing buuuuttt...) when your idea to make a fight with basic enemies hard/challenging is to just swarm the player with an extreme amount of enemies (im on the 2nd to hardest difficulty for context) it isnt truly challenging because its artificial inflation for a challenge. Modern cod zombies is guilty of the same thing in high rounds that instead as the higher in the round you go the more challenging enemies appear, now its just you go high rounds and get swarmed there is ABSOLUTELY 0 fun to be had running around trying to make space all while having 10 diff enemies on my ♥♥♥♥. I most recently got my kyber crystal of Ilum. tell me why EA you decide to spawn 2 purge troopers with electro staffs, 2 scout trooper commanders with electro staffs, 2 k series security droids, and 2 basic scout troopers with electro staffs all within one tinny teeeennnny ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ corner. That isnt hard or challenging fight when if i do try to fight i just get stun lock cycle in 1 corner by all of them in essence dying before i could even register what happened. 

Now that i got that out of my system i highly do recommend this game, the characters are all very memorable to myself at least. The enemy design, especially for the storm troopers i do like as they all stand out from one another. Visually for a game that came out in 2019 so rounding up hella about a decade now it still looks very nice. furthermore when the combat isnt artificially challenging it actually is quiet enjoyable.
its really fun to play and the story line is one of the best out there i cant wait to try jedi surviver

Much like Doom Eternal, this game contains excessive platforming that can be quite frustrating at times. 
I do not appreciate having every map containing 5-8 3D Mario mini games, with a lightsaber. 🤷‍♂️
Otherwise it's a fine game... I wish Steam had nonbinary reviews.
story was enjoyable, the gameplay left much to be desired for

I uninstalled the game after a few hours. Decent game. But it is a severly unoptimized pc game. Epic traversal stutter, inexplicable fps drops from 180fps to below 30fps after playing a few minutes.  Pausing the game for 3 seconds, and resuming again, instantly fixes it every time. But pausing too long, causes fps to drop to 15fps again. So you have to pause the game quickly, after every few minutes of play.

A really good 9/10 game
Completed it on Jedi Master difficulty setting with a score of 86% completion.
Combat is excellent and similar to the Witcher 3 system.
Gameplay is like a Dark Souls combined with Tomb raider, It isn't an open world game but the level design is really interesting,
I would highly recommend this game.

This is one of the best stories that Star Wars has to offer. Every character is well written and has their arc fleshed out well throughout the game. It's a compelling story that keeps you hooked throughout, combined with the great soulslike combat and metroidvania style of exploration that make this game truly a gem
game was really good pushed many stromtroopers of the cliff got an achievement for it so basically 10/10

Even though it wasn't quite as open world as I had hoped, the story and the progression kept me engaged. By the end of the game I was bordering on addiction. I do wish the lightsaber battles between melee opponents was a little more varied and dynamic, but overall I loved the game!

I got this game for 3 bucks on sale. I would buy this for full price in a heartbeat. Combat, story and environments are on point. You really feel like a jedi in a movie! Also the interactive cutscenes and cutscenes overall are phenomenal.
cool game, mods make it alot more harder for me personally. i think the base game is easy while playing on hardest difficulty
it is a really good star wars game but puzzles are literally terrible only reason they are in the game is to increase play time

One of the best Star Wars Stories to come out in recent times, and easily the best Star Wars game at the time of release. The Mantis crew recaptures the same charm and genuine characters we had in the Original Trilogy, and the authentic worlds, story, and setting really do make it something truly special.
Good game, but it's a shame they never fixed the stuttering issues

You have to download and make an EA account just to play, so inconvenient and annoying, I was literally gonna go and replay it but this bs made it way too much of a hassle, it also isn't the greatest gameplay wise either on top of the rest

Impressive graphics, interesting story and gameplay
A must play for any star wars fan

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is the Star Wars game I always wanted.

The lightsaber combat feels satisfying, exploring different planets is genuinely fun, and the game perfectly captures the feeling of being a Jedi trying to survive after Order 66. It mixes exploration, platforming, puzzles, and combat really well.

Cal Kestis starts off simple, but his journey becomes surprisingly emotional as the story progresses. The environments, music, and atmosphere feel straight out of a Star Wars movie.

The combat can be challenging at times, but mastering parries and Force abilities makes every fight rewarding. And yes — using a lightsaber never stops feeling cool.

A fantastic single-player Star Wars experience with a great story and fun gameplay.

Definitely worth playing for any Star Wars fan.

After finding a workaround for EA not allowing me to play the game for contravening some rule of theirs which they won't even tell me what I did wrong. I finally got to play this stupid game.  It's amusing in parts, the game play reminds me of Tomb Raider. The Star Wars scenario is good, but the combat is awful,  and then they completely ruin the game with an impossible "Ice slide" which requires perfect timing and sends you back to the start with every failure. What has sliding down some ice got to do with Star Wars?
I give up.
this game is utterly amazing i had so much fun playing it and for my first steam game i did not leave it dissapointed. i give it 8/10

This game made me finally realize that I HATE semi-open world games. This weird highly restrictive map that doesn't feel like I should be allowed to freely traverse is just wrong and boring. Not to mention the lack of fast travel, which depending on the game is a perfectly fine thing to do but it was a miss in this game. There were certain bosses that had frustrating walk backs if I died and had to return to a respawn point. Not to mention the respawning enemies mean I am fighting the same enemies every time I walk through any area. Exploration wasn't rewarding, I only got cosmetic options when I found a chest (a chest that was always extremely out of place with its environment might I add) and the literal only time I obtained something that was actually cool was the double bladed lightsaber upgrade, which was an upgrade I got literally right next to one of the main story progression points so it doesn't really count. 

The story was alright, the game itself is short and New Game Plus is a waste of time. You can start the game again with all of your customization options still unlocked but your skill points get reset for god knows whatever reason, meaning NG+ has no actual point since I still have to get all my abilities back. The only reason you would do NG+ is for the Meditation Combat Challenges but, again, all of my skills I obtained over the course of the game were reset. I'm not grinding through the whole game again just to do some combat challenges. Combat itself only felt good AFTER I obtained most if not all of the skill upgrades, a lot of the time combat felt frustrating to play (not difficult, frustrating) and I often lowered the difficulty because I just wanted to progress the game quicker.

If you played any of the new Tomb Raider games and liked them you will like this game, I don't agree with people saying the game is "Dark Souls but Star Wars" because frankly it bears far more similarity with the Tomb Raider series than that.
This is a very fun game. If a part is too hard, you can lower the difficulty. I recommend.
Still one of my favorite games. Enough i bought it for my roommate's playstation then again for myself.

Bit of a random purchase - not sure how much depth there is, but I'm really enjoying the gameplay so far - but it's star wars and you have a light sabre!

Some of the parkour elements are a little cluncky compared to other games but using the force to run along walls is pretty cool.

Jedi Fallen Order is absolutely phenomenal. I've been playing for around 9 hours now and the story has me in a chokehold. Unfortunately, I have to jump through way too many hoops to play using a controller, since EA insists on using their launcher for the game, as well as its sequel (♥♥♥♥ you by the way, EA). The graphics are phenomenal, each planet has a unique atmosphere, and the combat is one of the most fun things I've played as of recent. A must-buy for any Star Wars fan.
good story tellling and graphics...

It's clunky and frustrating and I don't know WHY Enemies have to respawn every time you heal. Most of your fetches are pretty unrewarding, But swinging a lightsaber is awesome. And the story is actually incredible.

Epic story, amazing lightsaber gameplay, i recommend It. It Is a bit tedious to 100%, so that's why i havent done it. Those crates are hidden everywhere, and there Is no tool in the game (as far as i know) that makes It easier to find them. You can probably search a map from Google or something though. Anyways that was a reeeally minor complaint by me, the game Is still amazing and 100% worth playing, even if you are not a star wars fan (yet)

Terrible game honestly. From the launcher being a pain to use on Steam Deck to the poor graphics and performance the game kept taking me out of the (mediocre) story. And seriously, out of every genre in gaming, Souls-Like is the worst possible one for Star Wars.
Great game. There is a lot of weight behind the hits and its really satisfying.

A fun and semi challenging souls like Star Wars experience. I really enjoy the story of Cal and the other main characters and cast, There are some really good references and pulls from the Star Wars mythos that I enjoyed, very excellent storyline and writing. The combat is really fun but a lot of this game is map exploration, which I think is really cool and can be really fun but I also tend to get really lost and impatient with running back and forth constantly until I hit an objective, it is a big personal gripe because I do know a lot of people who excel in that type of gameplay more than I. There are some areas where there is noticeable performance issues, but it never crashed or froze up completely, all in all it is a very satisfying and fun game and story to experience, definitely worth picking up whenever it’s on sale.

Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order was an enjoyable experience from beginning to end. The gameplay remained interesting thanks to its mix of combat, exploration, puzzle-solving, and character progression. The worlds were vast, deeply interconnected, and filled with both stunning sights and terrifying creatures, making exploration rewarding.

The characters we meet throughout our journey are interesting and flawed, fitting a story centered around the fall of the Jedi Order. This is a tale of loss, survival, and hope, as Cal Kestis works to gather the shattered pieces of the Order and find a path forward.

The variety of enemies keeps encounters engaging. Players face everything from standard Imperial stormtroopers and dangerous wildlife to the relentless Inquisitors. The game also delivers some memorable moments involving the terrifying presence of Darth Vader, reminding players just how powerful the Empire truly is.

Overall, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order is a great game with an engaging story, enjoyable combat, memorable characters, and excellent world design. I would happily play it again and recommend it to both Star Wars fans and action-adventure players.

I really enjoyed this game. The story and the characters were far better than I thought they would be. The combat is very satisfying, although a little janky at times. As far as souls-likes go, it's not super difficult even on grand-master as they give you a lot of room for mistakes.

One of the best Star Wars games released in the last decade, this title delivers excellent writing, natural and heartfelt dialogue, memorable character design, and fluid, engaging combat. Set within the familiar Star Wars galaxy, it tells a completely new and compelling story that feels fresh while remaining true to the franchise's spirit.

The game also excels visually, featuring stunning environments, beautifully designed planets, and impressive graphics that bring the Star Wars universe to life. Each location feels distinct and immersive, making exploration rewarding in its own right.

Even casual Star Wars fans are likely to enjoy what this game has to offer. My only minor criticisms are the occasional backtracking, which could have been reduced or eliminated entirely, and the abundance of collectible "slop" scattered throughout the world. Fortunately, these collectibles are purely cosmetic, so players won't miss any meaningful content by ignoring them.

These small issues do little to detract from what is otherwise a thoughtfully crafted and genuinely enjoyable experience. Overall, it's a standout title that captures the heart and adventure of Star Wars exceptionally well.

A really cool game with a very pleasant and immersive atmosphere. The music is also great. The combat is intricate in a good way, but sometimes annoying. The story is also not bad and easy to understand, but sometimes delivered through text databanks. I highly recommend this game if  you are a Star Wars fan, but I think that it is also a good way of getting into it, because it explains Order 66 nicely, partly by making you experience it yourself. I also need to add that in my opinion, is it a must-try if it is on sale.

the game is fine, if you like star wars get it when it is on sale.

Everything just feels clunky, saber combat, platforming, force powers.

Overall, this is a great game. I enjoy the combat gameplay, particularly the lightsaber and force mechanics. The story is interesting as it fills the story gaps between Star Wars Episodes 3 and 4. The world is semi-open and the graphics are beautiful. The only downside of the semi-open world is that it feels linear when going for all the achievements (sometimes requiring you to backtrack very far), though completing them all is very satisfying. The puzzle difficulty is easy or decent.  I played on Jedi Master difficulty, which is just a little bit challenging but still enjoyable. The only frustration was with the larger bosses, as the game can get a bit buggy (I occasionally got stuck inside a boss's body or the boss became invisible when too close)

[9/10]

Great game, fun combat, well written characters.

Fun Star Wars Fix kind of game. Action and platforming is fun. Puzzles can be challenging but rewarding.

Despite being an EA game, it is surprisingly good. There are little to no microtransactions, no bloat, and no stupid multiplayer ♥♥♥♥. It offers a solid single-player experience, about 30 hours long, with great visuals and a nice story.

The combat can be a little frustrating, since they prioritized difficulty over logic and consistency. I found it particularly frustrating having to hit enemies with my lightsaber so many times before they die, when in lore they are supposed to die basically instantly. I understand they did it so the game would have some challenge, but on the higher difficulties, this challenge turns into pure frustration.

Instead of enemies becoming smarter or better in combat, the game simply makes it harder to land counters, perfect dodges, or perfect blocks, while giving the enemies more health. Because the visuals of the combat remain exactly the same, the difficulty change is purely artificial. You find yourself frustrated because you see Cal hitting an enemy with a clean attack that should split them in two, yet they remain unfazed. This creates a frustrating mismatch in your brain, which expects to see lightsabers sever limbs and cut through anything with ease. It ends up making you feel like you are fighting with a crude stick instead of a sophisticated weapon.

Either way, the story is solid, the game is good, and if you like Star Wars, I believe you will enjoy it. The price has come down significantly since launch and it goes on sale frequently, so wait a bit and you will get it for a really solid price.

When I first got the game it had its share of bugs and glitches. However those glitches seem to have been taken care of. This game is an amazing expansion on the Star Wars franchise and I cannot wait to see what other adventures Cal and friends will go in in the future.
Its an excellent representation of the star wars universe I love it.
game was just perfect except that there was no fast travel and the maps are very confusing, especially Dathomir

Looks great, plays awful. How this game gets overwhelming positive reviews is beyond me. Whole lotta Star Wars fans, for sure! 

The lightsaber combat, which should be amazing, is reduced to button mashing with some basic positioning. Near zero skill required. Constant back tracking is a total chore. The "squeeze through the crack in the wall" gimmick is overused on the first world never mind the remainder of the game. Don't get me started on the god awful swimming mechanics...

Which is all terrible because this game has good story line, decent character development. It feels like it needs a month with the FromSoftware team that did Sekiro and this game would be cracked. Alas, it's that kind of disappointment that is simply so close to being AWESOME. They **almost** had it.

EA should have just made a parcours game without the random slowdowns during what is normally extremely fast paced combat. They are trying to copy ubisofts assassins creed combat and that series should have ended after the first edition

First time being a Jedi Knight myself, and completed the game on the Jedi Master difficulty, I must say that I do not understand why they don't make these SW single player games like this anymore. Fallen order was beautiful joyride with a decent story and world building. However, I still cannot stand the fact that the game does not have fast travel by any mean, and therefore traversing in this game for cleaning up collectibles is an absolute nightmare, but it brought total satisfaction after finishing everything.
